body.para: Once machine installation and startup are completed, you are ready to start machine operation. Generally, Makino installation staff are present at the installation site until the customer can start actual machining, but the safety information and work items for starting operation are presented below, and so please use them as a checklist.

list.li: Connect the main power supply and conduct power test
Connect the pneumatic source and check the pressure
Check and fill with hydraulic oil
Check and fill with cooling oil
Check and fill with cutting fluid
Check the feed axis lubricating grease
Check the alarm display after the power is turned on, and check the status (unusual noises or fluid leaks) of each machine component
Check operation of the safety devices (emergency stop buttons, door interlock, etc.)
Check the basic operation (spindle rotation, axis feeding, and various auxiliary functions besides the automatic tool changer (ATC))
Check the reference position of the tool magazine
Check the alignment of the tool magazine (Confirm Section 8 “Centering Alignment” in Chapter 6 of Maintenance Manual (H020I-M1E-XA) to make adjustment)

body.para: After the above checks are completed, register the tool data to the machine based on the customer's management method. A program tool number can be registered to the tools that are used for enabling use of the automatic tool change function (M6 and T functions).

A program number with the same number as each pod (tool storage location) in the tool magazine is already registered at shipping, and so the tool change operation can still be performed automatically even if no tool numbers are registered. To use your own unique tool number, register, and then perform the next tool related operations.

list.li: Check the automatic tool changer (ATC) related auxiliary functions (M6 operations, T functions)
Check the coolant nozzle discharge direction
